title: dryas octopetala subsp. hookeriana
intro: Dryas octopetala subsp. hookeriana, also known as Dryad's saddle or mountain avens, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dryad's saddle include potential anti-arthritic and anti-aging effects, as well as support for digestive health. Herbal preparations of Dryad's saddle include infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
